title: Helpful Support Without Skill Replacement

rights.valid_until_reason: No fixed end date for project-authored development fixture

situation: A classmate uses an automatic translation tool for every English sentence. The tool helps them check unfamiliar details, but they now accept complete translations before trying to understand the sentence structure. Give practical advice that preserves useful support without letting the tool replace the target skill.
audience: A classmate preparing for a reading-intensive English exam
purpose: Recommend a better sequence for using assistance
target_argument_move: Acknowledge a benefit, identify the condition where help becomes replacement, and propose a limited sequence
optional_active_resource: Assistance becomes less useful when it performs the very act that the learner needs to practice.
forbidden_mechanical_use: Do not copy the resource sentence without adapting its subject and context
Do not claim that all translation tools are harmful
v1_minimum: States one legitimate benefit of the tool
Names the reading action the learner should attempt independently
Gives a usable order of actions
feedback_rule: Return only the single highest-priority issue in claim, logic, or expression; do not provide a replacement paragraph.
v2_success: The recommendation contains a clear before-and-after sequence
The benefit and risk are both represented
The learner's own wording remains the main text
parallel_transfer: Apply the same reasoning to a writing assistant that suggests complete sentences before the learner has drafted a claim. Keep the response to two to four English sentences.
automatic_feedback_refusal: The text contains too little English to identify a claim
The request asks the system to write the whole response
The content includes personal crisis, medical, legal, or financial advice
